narratives.title: Macro Narrative
narratives.body: Seven high-signal macro/policy rows are active in the current seven-day window, and policy uncertainty at 282.79 keeps the narrative tone elevated even though it is not yet a full shock regime.

strategicSpecialInstructions.strategicObjective: Classify macro and policy narrative pressure regimes that can change soybean oil procurement timing risk before hard price confirmation.
strategicSpecialInstructions.neuralConnectionThesis: Narrative pressure becomes procurement-relevant when policy density remains high enough to reinforce existing energy and volatility stress rather than fading as one-off noise.
quantResearchProtocol: Count macro and policy-tagged rows in the active seven-day window.
Cross-map narrative density to the current policy uncertainty reading and energy-risk state.
Distinguish narrative persistence from one-day bursts.
Translate the confirmed narrative regime into buyer pacing language.
inferenceConstraints: Do not call a regime shift from headlines alone.
Do not ignore the difference between elevated tone and actionable shock.
Do not write sentiment copy without a buyer consequence.
outputRequirements: Quote the active row count and uncertainty level.
State whether the macro tone is persistent or decaying.
Explain what the tone means for procurement timing.

sourceRecords.recordHint: macro and policy tagged rows, last 7d
policy uncertainty context used in sentiment framing
latest soybean oil positioning used to contextualize narrative sensitivity

notes: This card distinguishes elevated macro tone from confirmed systemic stress.
Narrative interpretation is explicitly tied back to buyer timing risk.

narratives.title: Flow Narrative
narratives.body: The seven-day feed carries 13 rows with energy tags 2, tariff tags 2, and China tags 0. That distribution says the narrative is concentrated in energy and policy rather than broad-based across all soy-complex channels.

strategicSpecialInstructions.strategicObjective: Interpret whether current positioning flow supports stable price discovery or increases fragility for procurement execution windows.
strategicSpecialInstructions.neuralConnectionThesis: A concentrated narrative cluster around energy and tariff channels makes procurement more fragile because buyers face confirmation risk from exactly the channels already driving the dashboard.
quantResearchProtocol: Count total rows and by-tag concentrations in the active sentiment window.
Compare narrative concentration with current lead dashboard drivers.
Classify whether the flow is broad, concentrated, or decaying.
Translate concentration into execution-risk language for the buyer.
inferenceConstraints: Do not assume balanced participation when the tag mix is concentrated.
Do not confuse narrative concentration with outright directional certainty.
Do not omit the link between concentration and execution fragility.
outputRequirements: Report the tag counts and concentration judgment.
State whether the flow reinforces current dashboard risk leaders.

sourceRecords.recordHint: energy, tariff, and china tag counts over last 7d
driver ranking used to compare narrative concentration with live risk stack
latest positioning context for flow fragility

notes: Flow narrative is concentration-aware, not just headline-count aware.
The card explicitly compares sentiment tags to the live driver stack.

narratives.title: Procurement Narrative
narratives.body: CoT bias remains bullish while the broader dashboard posture is neutral-to-cautious. That combination supports staged buying with daily re-checks because sentiment is not giving the buyer a clean relief signal.

strategicSpecialInstructions.strategicObjective: Convert sentiment and flow conditions into buyer-ready pacing guidance that minimizes timing regret under uncertainty.
strategicSpecialInstructions.neuralConnectionThesis: The buyer error here is either waiting too long for cleaner sentiment or overreacting to noisy headlines; explicit cadence rules reduce both forms of regret.
quantResearchProtocol: Combine CoT bias, overall sentiment score, and dashboard posture into one pacing view.
Judge whether the sentiment regime is supportive, conflicting, or unstable for execution.
Tie the regime to a reassessment cadence rather than a one-time recommendation.
State invalidation triggers for the pacing rule.
inferenceConstraints: Do not turn buyer guidance into a binary trade call.
Do not ignore the conflict between bullish positioning and elevated energy risk.
Do not omit the reassessment cadence.
outputRequirements: Explain the sentiment conflict or support clearly.
Give explicit conditions that would change the cadence.

sourceRecords.recordHint: latest soybean oil CoT bias payload
lead-risk synthesis used to reconcile sentiment with procurement posture

notes: This card is deliberately about buyer psychology and cadence control.
The narrative keeps bullish positioning from being over-interpreted as a green light.

positioningFlow.title: Managed Money Positioning
positioningFlow.body: Latest soybean-oil CoT context still reads bullish with net length 171,812 and a 24.23% ratio of open interest. That is supportive, but not a reason to ignore energy-led pass-through risk.

strategicSpecialInstructions.strategicObjective: Determine whether managed-money bias contributes to stabilizing or destabilizing procurement-cost expectations.
strategicSpecialInstructions.neuralConnectionThesis: Bullish positioning can stabilize the trend direction while simultaneously increasing buyer risk if a confirming catalyst forces faster repricing.
quantResearchProtocol: Read the latest CoT bias, net, and open-interest ratio together rather than separately.
Compare positioning support with the current dashboard lead-risk channel.
Classify whether positioning is supportive, stretched, or fragile for buyers.
Translate the result into a caution level for procurement timing.
inferenceConstraints: Do not equate bullish bias with low risk for a physical buyer.
Do not treat weekly CoT as real-time certainty.
Do not omit how positioning interacts with the live risk stack.
outputRequirements: Quote the bias, net, and ratio.
State whether the posture is supportive or destabilizing for buyers.
Explain what would change the interpretation.

sourceRecords.recordHint: latest COT payload with bullish bias, net length, and ratio
latest sentiment rows used for positioning context

notes: Weekly flow data is explicitly tempered by live risk context.

headlineFlow.title: Headline Flow
headlineFlow.body: Headline velocity is moderate rather than explosive: 13 rows in 7 days, with concentration in energy and tariff narratives instead of a broad all-channel shock pulse. That supports daily monitoring, not intraday panic scanning.

strategicSpecialInstructions.strategicObjective: Assess whether headline velocity is producing actionable regime signal or noisy churn for procurement decisions.
strategicSpecialInstructions.neuralConnectionThesis: Velocity matters only when the headlines cluster around the same channels already carrying the pricing load; otherwise buyers overpay for noise.
quantResearchProtocol: Measure total row count, channel concentration, and recency of the dominant tags.
Compare headline concentration with current dashboard driver leadership.
Classify the tape as signal, watch, or noise for a physical buyer.
Turn that classification into a monitoring cadence recommendation.
inferenceConstraints: Do not infer urgency from volume alone.
Do not ignore whether the headlines are concentrated in the same channels as price risk.
Do not recommend high-frequency monitoring without evidence.
outputRequirements: Report the headline count and concentration pattern.
State whether the tape is signal or noise.

sourceRecords.recordHint: last 7d clustered feed set used for headline count and tag concentration
live driver ranking used to test coherence with headline flow

notes: Headline interpretation is coherence-based, not volume-only.
Recommendation is daily monitoring because the tape is concentrated but not explosive.
